org.arl.fjage.groovy

Class FSMBuilder

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.arl.fjage.Behavior
      • org.arl.fjage.FSMBehavior
        • org.arl.fjage.groovy.FSMBuilder
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Comparable<Behavior>

    public class FSMBuilder
extends FSMBehavior
    Finite state machine (FSM) builder.

    While fjåge provides a FSMBehavior class to help with making FSMs, this class adds some Groovy functionality to make it simpler to describe FSMs in Groovy.

    The usage of this class is best described through an example FSM: 

     enum State { TICK, TOCK }
 enum Event { RESET }

 FSMBehavior fsm = FSMBuilder.build {

   long count = 0

   state(State.TICK) {
     onEnter {
       println 'TICK'
     }
     after(1) {                     // every time we enter the TICK state,
       setNextState(State.TOCK)     //   switch to TOCK after 1 second
     }
     action {
       println "CLOCK says $count seconds"
       block()
     }
     onEvent(Event.RESET) {         // reset count if RESET trigger received
       count = 0
     }
   }

   state(State.TOCK) {
     onEnter {
       println 'TOCK'
       after(1) {                  // in this form, the delay is recomputed every
         setNextState(State.TICK)  //   time the TOCK state is entered
       }
     }
     onEvent(Event.RESET) {         // reset count if RESET trigger received
       count = 0                    //   and also go back to TICK state
       setNextState(State.TICK)
     }
     onExit {                       // increment count every time we leave
       count++                      //   the TICK state
     }
   }

 }
    The DSL may also be used in statically compiled (@CompileStatic) Groovy code. The delay passed to after() is a number of seconds.

    This class is implemented in Java (rather than Groovy) intentionally: the Groovy compiler adds synthetic propertyMissing/methodMissing bridge methods to nested Groovy classes, and the static type checker then resolves any unqualified property in a delegating closure against those bridges (as Object), breaking access to fields of the enclosing class in statically compiled FSM definitions.
